title: oxalis leucolepis
intro: Oxalis leucolepis, also known as the wood sorrel or creeping wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, Oxalidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anthelmintic and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Oxalis leucolepis include its ability to act as a natural antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic, making it useful for treating a range of conditions such as urinary tract infections and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Oxalis leucolepis can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
